channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Tier 1 geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷⚔️ US forces board Iranian oil tanker, Trump orders navy to shoot boats laying mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US military intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade. Trump declared the US has "total control" over the Strait of Hormuz and authorized lethal force against any boats laying mines.

item.title: 🇱🇧📰💔 Israel kills Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil, drawing international condemnation
item.description: Lebanese PM called the attack a "war crime" after journalist Amal Khalil was killed in what officials described as a "double-tap" strike. Her death has prompted fresh claims about Israel targeting media workers.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦💰 EU formally approves €90 billion Ukraine loan and 20th Russia sanctions package
item.description: The European Union gave final approval to a massive €90 billion loan package for Ukraine, heavily weighted toward military spending, reflecting the view that peace in Ukraine remains distant.

item.title: 🇮🇳🗳️ Millions in India stripped of vote before critical West Bengal state election
item.description: Experts say Muslims and other minorities have been disproportionately deleted from electoral rolls ahead of the West Bengal elections, as the government seeks to "purify" voter lists.

item.title: 🚨🌊 About 250 Rohingya refugees missing after boat capsizes in Andaman Sea
item.description: A boat carrying Rohingya refugees capsized in the Andaman Sea, leaving approximately 250 people missing in one of the deadliest maritime incidents involving the persecuted minority in recent months.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Orders Military to Shoot Iranian Mine-Laying Boats in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump authorized the US Navy to "shoot and kill" any Iranian boats deploying mines to choke traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, escalating the US-Iran standoff. The order comes as Iran attacked three cargo ships and captured two in the strait following Trump's ceasefire extension announcement.

item.title: 🇵🇭 ICC Confirms Crimes Against Humanity Trial for Former Philippine President Duterte
item.description: The International Criminal Court confirmed all charges against Rodrigo Duterte for his "war on drugs" that killed thousands, committing the 81-year-old former president to trial. Judges said there were substantial grounds to believe he played a key role in 76 murders.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Navy Secretary John Phelan Abruptly Departs Pentagon
item.description: The Pentagon announced Navy Secretary John Phelan is leaving his position with no reason given, the first head of a military service to depart during Trump's second term. The departure comes amid the Navy's blockade of Iranian ports and targeting of Iranian-linked ships worldwide.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Senate Advances $70 Billion Funding for ICE and Border Patrol
item.description: The Senate voted 50-48 early Thursday to advance funding for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol, taking the first steps toward reopening the Department of Homeland Security. The department has been partially shut down since mid-February.

item.title: 🇹🇿 More Than 500 Killed in Tanzania Election Violence, Inquiry Finds
item.description: A commission of inquiry reported that 518 people died from "unnatural causes" following protests after Tanzania's disputed election where President Samia Suluhu Hassan won with 98% of the vote. Opposition parties accused security forces of a brutal crackdown.

item.title: 🇺🇦 12 Russian FSB Officers Killed in Drone Strike on Donetsk Command Post
item.description: Ukrainian forces struck a Russian FSB command post in occupied Donetsk using FP-2 drones, killing 12 officers. The drones were manufactured by Ukrainian defense company Fire Point with 60-100kg payloads.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Formally Approves 90 Billion Euro Ukraine Loan, 20th Russia Sanctions Package
item.description: The EU gave final approval to a massive 90 billion euro loan for Ukraine alongside its 20th sanctions package against Russia. Zelensky said the funds will strengthen Ukraine's army and enable social obligations.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Uses Subsidized Mortgages to Drive Population Replacement in Occupied Mariupol
item.description: Russian authorities are drawing new Russian buyers to occupied Mariupol with subsidized mortgages while thousands of Ukrainians whose homes were destroyed or seized remain without housing.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Fires on 3 Ships in Strait of Hormuz After Trump Extends Ceasefire
item.description: Iranian forces fired on three vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian media reported the MSC Francesca and Epaminondas were being escorted to Iran, raising concerns about continued tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran's Exiled Crown Prince Splashed with Red Substance in Berlin
item.description: Reza Pahlavi was targeted while leaving a press conference where he said diplomacy with Iran "has been given enough chance." The incident highlights tensions among Iranian opposition figures.

item.title: ⚓ Trump's Top Navy Official Out After Reported Clashes at Pentagon
item.description: John Phelan's abrupt departure comes as the US Navy enforces a high-stakes blockade of Iran and follows the firing of a top US Army general. The Pentagon provided no reason for the move.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Seizes Second Iranian Oil Tanker as Strait of Hormuz Tensions Escalate
item.description: The US Navy has seized a second tanker carrying Iranian oil, with destroyers now shadowing additional vessels that departed Iran before the US-imposed blockade began. President Trump has ordered the Navy to shoot any boats placing mines in the strategic waterway.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Now Run by Military Generals After Khamenei Killing
item.description: The killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has ushered in a new collective leadership in Iran, with the Revolutionary Guards assuming greater power over the countrys political direction.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany Breaks With Israel Over Iran War Conduct
item.description: German officials have issued critical comments about Israel in recent weeks, signaling Berlins growing frustration with its longtime ally amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇧🇬 Bulgarias Pro-Russia Leader Faces West-East Choice After Election Win
item.description: Rumen Radev, known for pro-Russia statements, handily won Bulgarias presidential election. Now both Bulgarians and EU partners wait to see whether he will align with Moscow or Brussels.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putins Approval Ratings Drop as Internet Restrictions Frustrate Russians
item.description: Expanding state restrictions on the Internet are making daily life difficult for millions of Russian citizens, sending President Putins approval ratings on a rare downward trajectory.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 China Rejects US Criticism Over Taiwan Flight Pressure
item.description: Beijing dismissed Washington's criticism after the US accused China of using intimidation to block Taiwan President Lai Ching-te's flight permits to Africa. The diplomatic spat highlights escalating tensions over Taiwan's international space.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU Approves €106 Billion Loan for Ukraine, Signaling Long War Ahead
item.description: The European Union approved a massive €106 billion loan package for Ukraine, reflecting Brussels' assessment that peace with Russia remains distant. The loan is backed by profits from frozen Russian assets.

item.title: 🇺🇸📊 Pew Poll Shows Americans Losing Confidence in Trump's Ukraine Handling
item.description: A new Pew Research Center survey reveals declining American confidence in President Trump's approach to the Russia-Ukraine war, with growing skepticism about US strategy as the conflict enters its third year.

item.title: 🇷🇺⚔️ Russian Battlefield Gains Halt, Worst Progress in Two Years
item.description: Moscow's forces have made minimal territorial gains in recent weeks, with analysts describing it as their worst progress in two years. Ukrainian defensive positions have stabilized despite ongoing Russian assaults.
